title: Request Authority, Host, Target Reconstruction, and Routing · Runtime Contract: Absolute Form Host Consistency
summary: A server trusts any Host, accepts duplicate authority sources, reconstructs links from forwarded input, routes before validating the target, and confuses TLS identity with request authority. Planned artifact: Add and verify the absolute form host consistency increment in authority validation and target reconstruction table. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Handle proxy absolute-form authority and Host according to RFC rules while rejecting unsafe disagreement.
Reconstruct the effective request URI from target form, request-target, Host, connection security, and trusted configuration.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Absolute Form Host Consistency · Theory Absolute Form Host Consistency
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 8790 in a regional status service with slow assistive and low-bandwidth clients. Build deterministic Go evidence to derive and allowlist one effective request authority without trusting disagreement; preserve this operating constraint: diagnostics must omit credentials and attacker-controlled metric labels; then block extra QPACK streams and fall back without removing HTTP/2 availability. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Absolute Form Host Consistency contract for the supplied data.
